description_long: This is a rustic, wooden tankard or bucket with a polygonal, slightly flared body constructed from vertical wooden staves. The exterior is reinforced by two thick, dark metal bands—one near the top and one near the bottom—each fastened with prominent, gold-colored rivets. A large, angular handle made of the same dark metal is attached to one side, providing a sturdy grip. The wood grain is clearly visible, giving the object a handcrafted, medieval or fantasy appearance. The interior appears hollow, suitable for holding liquids or small items, and the top is open with no visible lid. The overall design suggests durability and functionality, likely intended for use in a tavern, street market, or outdoor urban setting.
description: A wooden tankard or bucket with dark metal bands and a large angular handle, featuring visible wood grain and gold rivets. The top is open and the object has a rustic, handcrafted look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, wood staves, metal bands, and the full angular handle on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the uninterrupted wood staves and both metal bands, with the handle not visible.
description_view_2: Angled view showing the open top, wood texture, and a partial view of the handle on the right.
description_view_3: Rear angled view focusing on the handle's attachment and the back of the bucket, with metal bands and rivets visible.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops with gold rivets at top and bottom
